context.story_engine: Medical staff at a Chicago emergency room face life-and-death decisions, each experiencing their aspect of healing, ambition, and personal sacrifice

plotlines.name: Mark: Marriage
plotlines.goal: save his marriage while pursuing his medical career
plotlines.obstacle: competing career demands, geographic separation, Jennifer's resentment

plotlines.name: Susan: Competence
plotlines.goal: prove herself as a capable physician
plotlines.obstacle: Dr. Kayson's attacks on her medical judgment
plotlines.stakes: career destruction, loss of medical license

plotlines.name: Doug: Love
plotlines.obstacle: his own self-destructive patterns, unresolved feelings for Carol
plotlines.stakes: perpetual loneliness, inability to form family

plotlines.name: Carol: Choice
plotlines.obstacle: conflicted feelings, fear of making wrong decision

plotlines.name: Peter: Ambition
plotlines.obstacle: competition, family obligations to his mother
plotlines.stakes: career stagnation, loss of surgical opportunities

plotlines.name: Carter: Belonging
plotlines.goal: prove himself worthy of a medical career
plotlines.obstacle: inexperience, competition from other students, self-doubt
plotlines.stakes: failure in medicine, loss of purpose

events.event: Mark weighs a lucrative private practice offer while Jennifer pushes him to leave the ER
Carter arrives for his first rotation and is assigned to Benton's surgical service
Benton gives Carter a whirlwind orientation then disappears to his own cases
Carter treats a hand laceration and accidental gunshot wound
Carter becomes nauseated treating a stabbing victim and steps outside
Greene finds Carter outside and reassures him that emotional response makes him a good doctor
Doug suspects child abuse and presses the mother with pointed questions
Susan consoles a fragile cancer patient struggling with her diagnosis
Building collapse floods ER with dozen of critically injured victims
Benton encounters patient with ruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm
Benton operates solo for the first time, clamping the bleed until Morgenstern arrives
Morgenstern acknowledges both the risk and skill of Benton's solo surgery
Greene delivers death notification to man whose father died in collapse
Carol is brought to ER after overdosing on pills in suicide attempt
Doug is visibly shaken by Carol's suicide attempt
Team works frantically to stabilize Carol
Greene leaves hospital with private practice offer still unresolved

interactions.description: all storylines explore what characters sacrifice for their commitments - Mark's marriage for medicine, Benton's safety for advancement, Carter's comfort for belonging, Carol's life itself
Carol's suicide attempt forces Doug to confront his feelings and impacts her choice between him and Tag
Mark counsels Carter about keeping emotional response while suppressing his own feelings about his failing marriage

patches.reason: Building collapse creates major plot event that doesn't advance any character's personal goal but drives the episode structure

events.event: Susan is roused from sleep in a supply closet by a nurse
Susan discovers an earring lodged in a baby's throat and removes it, restoring breathing
Susan clashes with a psychiatric attending over admitting a dementia patient
Susan spends the day lobbying every hospital department to take the elderly woman, being turned away at every turn
Mark and Jennifer celebrate her passing the bar exam
Mark and Jennifer are caught having sex in an ER storage room after triggering the emergency button
Jennifer hints that Mark never truly believed she would pass the bar
Mark becomes preoccupied with needing to return to the hospital
Carter misses his first trauma opportunity because Lydia lets him sleep through it
Carter cares for German tourists and wedding party with food poisoning from the Hofbrauhaus
Carter successfully defibrillates Mr. Zambano during cardiac arrest, his first successful code
Carter treats a flirtatious young woman with a rash who climbs into his Jeep uninvited
Peter clashes with Dr. Langworthy over Carter's responsibilities and surgical assignments
A patient Benton originally diagnosed is discharged prematurely by another attending, then returns to the ER
Benton's surgical instincts are validated, reinforcing his ambition to advance
Doug finally visits Carol in recovery, eight weeks after her overdose
Carol's mother Helen is hostile when Doug appears
Carol accepts Doug's flowers and they talk briefly
Doug remains emotionally guarded, unable to articulate his feelings, and the visit ends without resolution
Mark treats an elderly woman whose husband is not ready to accept her decline

interactions.description: all three storylines explore characters proving their professional worth through crisis moments
all three storylines deal with relationships under strain and the difficulty of emotional connection
Doug's visit to Carol directly connects his romantic storyline with her recovery arc

patches.reason: Mark's treatment of the elderly woman and her husband represents a quiet medical storyline about accepting decline and mortality that doesn't fit existing character arcs

events.event: Carol returns to work at County General despite her mother's concerns
Doug sees Carol briefly and says hello, then asks her to lunch
Carol declines lunch because she has plans with Tag
Doug invites Carol for coffee and she accepts
Dr. Kayson disputes Susan's treatment of heart-attack patient Mr. Flannigan
Morgenstern moves up the M&M conference time so Kayson can attend
Susan gets little support from colleagues at the M&M conference
Morgenstern reprimands Kayson for failing to supervise the case
Morgenstern questions whether Susan is assertive enough for future challenges
Mark treats a beaten woman who refuses to report abuse or accept help
Carol cares for a dying leukemia patient who offers wise counsel about valuing life
Disoriented woman 'Madame X' is brought in singing nonstop
Leukemia patient recognizes the melodies and identifies Madame X as Mary Kavanaugh
Staff throws Carol a surprise welcome-back party
Carol delivers speech thanking everyone and promising to treat life as a gift
Doug watches Carol's speech with relief and lingering guilt

episodes.theme: the courage to heal and move forward

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of healing and growth - Carol from trauma, Susan from professional attack, Carol choosing her path forward, Mark confronting limits of helping others
the audience sees Doug's guilt and feelings while Carol navigates between him and Tag

events.event: Steven Tierney is brought in after a hit-and-run accident
Benton and Langworthy are unable to save Steven Tierney
Carter is assigned to identify the victim and notify parents
Carter contacts the wrong family and tells them their son is dead
Langworthy delivers news to wrong parents who reveal dead boy is not theirs
Carter must locate Steven's actual parents for real notification
Haleh tells Carter the hospital cannot save everyone
Doug treats young boy Ozzy whose schizophrenic mother is brought in
Doug promises Ozzy he will not be separated from his mother
Cvetic diagnoses the mother as delusional and recommends placement
Mother has psychotic episode and Ozzy runs away in panic
Doug tells Ozzy the truth about his mother going to psychiatric facility
Susan tells Mark she has begun dating Div Cvetic
Mark is surprised but supportive of Susan's relationship
Susan treats salesman whose cell phone interferes with equipment
Walt asks Benton to look after his mother Mae whose health is declining
Benton is too busy with surgical schedule to help with Mae
Walt is left to manage Mae alone
Former patient visits Mark with frozen steaks declaring Dr. Greene Day

interactions.description: all three storylines explore the painful gap between good intentions and harsh realities - Carter's mistake, Doug's broken promise, Benton's neglect of family
Susan's romantic relationship with Cvetic intersects with Doug's case when Cvetic makes the psychiatric diagnosis

patches.reason: Steven Tierney case doesn't fit existing storylines - it's a procedural medical case that catalyzes Carter's growth but isn't about his belonging per se

events.event: Mark and Susan fight to keep Samuel Gasner alive on the heart-transplant waiting list
Mark and Susan revive Samuel repeatedly, shocking his heart back into rhythm
No donor organ becomes available and Samuel dies despite their efforts
Samuel tells Mark to make every moment count with his family
Carol overhears and asks Mark if he'll tell her to be grateful to be alive
Mark handles Carol's pointed reference to her suicide attempt with quiet sensitivity
Jennifer tells Mark she's been offered a federal clerkship in Milwaukee
Jennifer wants Mark to look for a hospital position in Milwaukee
Mark suggests Jen commute instead of him leaving his chief residency
Jennifer points out commuting would mean even less time with Rachel
Doug treats a young girl with severe asthma whose mother cannot afford medication
Peter treats Ivan, a liquor-store owner who shot himself in the foot
Carter discovers his one-night stand Liz may have given him an STI
Carter approaches Doug for advice on how to test himself
Liz is seen pursuing Dr. Kayson, revealing her pattern of seducing multiple doctors

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different forms of commitment - to family, to patients, to life itself
Samuel's dying words about family time directly impact both Mark's marriage crisis and his sensitive handling of Carol's recovery

events.event: Air conditioning fails on the hottest day in October, overwhelming County General with patients from two closed ERs
Mark's babysitter's car breaks down, forcing him to bring daughter Rachel to the ER
Rachel witnesses the harsh realities of the ER while spending the day with Mark
Doug and Mark treat five-year-old Kanesha who swallowed cocaine
Doug initially suspects Kanesha's father is responsible for the cocaine
Drug test reveals Kanesha's older sister possessed the cocaine, not the father
Doug confides to the father that he has a son, revealing his complicated feelings about fatherhood
Ivan shoots and seriously wounds a teenage robber
Benton and Langworthy try in vain to save the fourteen-year-old boy
Detective reveals Ivan chased the unarmed teenager before shooting him
Chloe Lewis appears unannounced at the hospital for the first time
Chloe steals Susan's television and VCR, wreaking havoc
Doug shows up uninvited at Carol's apartment while she's with Tag
Doug crosses the line with provocative behavior toward Carol and Tag
Carol slugs Doug, drawing a clear boundary but revealing deep emotion
Doug meets Linda Farrell, an attractive pharmaceutical sales representative

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of responsibility - Mark to his daughter and marriage, Susan to her chaotic sister and patients, Carol choosing between two men, Doug confronting his feelings about fatherhood, Peter facing surgical failure
Doug's pursuit of Carol directly intersects with her choice between him and Tag

events.event: Peter Benton and Dr. Langworthy both interview for the prestigious Starzl Fellowship in surgery
Benton saves a man with a severe throat wound, earning praise from Dr. Morgenstern
Mark Greene walks Carter through a complex procedure successfully
Carter wins a bottle of champagne and gets three leads on apartments
Doug Ross and Carol Hathaway share a spontaneous kiss following a stressful trauma
Carol quickly backtracks and acts cool toward Doug for the rest of the shift
Carol has second thoughts about moving in with Tag but ultimately decides to go ahead
Susan Lewis's drunk sister Chloe crashes her birthday celebration at the hospital
Chloe throws a violent tantrum, lacerating her hand in a fit of anger
Div Cvetic steps in to calm Chloe down, demonstrating his psychiatric skills
Haleh confronts Benton about refusing to act as a role model for a young gang member
Benton resists taking on responsibilities outside of surgery
Jennifer Greene visits from Milwaukee but has to return sooner than expected
Both Mark and Jen feel the distance widening between them

interactions.description: all storylines explore what characters must sacrifice to achieve their goals - Benton's humanity for surgical success, Carol's feelings for stability, Mark's marriage for career, Carter's comfort for growth
the audience sees Doug's hope and disappointment while Carol struggles with her decision, neither fully understanding the other's perspective

events.event: Mark calls in sick to spend the day with Jennifer
Doug is left in charge of the ER for the full shift
Doug struggles with administrative demands and patient volume
Carol treats a rape victim who fears reporting the assault
Carol navigates the victim's fear of her abusive boyfriend finding out
Carol tries to persuade the woman to cooperate with police
Walt brings Mae to the ER with a sprained ankle
Peter realizes Mae's condition has deteriorated significantly
Peter learns he was not awarded the Starzl Fellowship
Cvetic shows signs of stress, lashing out at colleagues
Susan notices Cvetic's erratic behavior but doesn't intervene
New aide Bob joins the ER staff
Doug appreciates Mark's steady leadership by shift's end

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of responsibility - Mark to his marriage vs career, Peter to family vs ambition, Carol to vulnerable patients, Susan to troubled relationships, Doug learning leadership responsibility
the audience knows Mark is playing hooky while Doug struggles with his responsibilities

events.event: Carol faces ethical dilemma when car accident patient confesses he was the driver who killed a pedestrian
Carol tells Tag the truth about sleeping with Doug a month before her suicide attempt and kissing him two weeks ago
Tag does not take the news well and their engagement is strained
Carter treats Ms. Carlton, a transgender woman, for head lacerations from traffic accident
Staff treats Ms. Carlton with passive disregard after discovering her transgender status
Ms. Carlton climbs to the hospital roof in despair
Cvetic fails to talk Ms. Carlton down and she jumps to her death
Carter explodes at Benton in frustration, blaming him for not doing enough
Benton invites Carter to spend Thanksgiving dinner with his family
Carter accepts Benton's invitation having no other plans
Cvetic breaks under stress and stands in crowded street daring cars to hit him
Susan discovers Cvetic has quit his job and moved out without telling her
Doug treats a man pecked by a turkey
Doug feels uncomfortable with Linda controlling their relationship and paying for everything
Doug departs for Jamaican vacation with Linda despite his unease
Chloe announces to Susan that she is pregnant

episodes.theme: the weight of truth and consequences

interactions.description: all storylines explore the burden of truth - Carol's confession to Tag, Carter confronting institutional prejudice, Doug's discomfort with his kept status, Susan facing Chloe's pregnancy
Carter's emotional breakdown brings him and Benton together, transforming their mentor-student dynamic

patches.reason: Cvetic's mental breakdown and suicidal behavior represents a major storyline that doesn't fit existing character arcs

events.event: Jerry sings 'Jingle Bells' and staff engage in playful activities during the empty ER
Greene and Susan put a cast on Carter's leg as a practical joke
Carol announces her engagement to Tag, showing her ring to the staff
Doug returns from Jamaica with Linda and congratulates Carol on her engagement
Linda privately tells Carol that Doug is still in love with her
Dr. Angela Hicks arrives as new ER attending
Dispatch reports massive thirty-two-vehicle pileup on Kennedy Expressway
Staff immediately enacts disaster protocol and readies trauma supplies
Doug triages patients to different trauma rooms including severe burns and spinal injuries
Susan struggles to save a patient who develops an aneurysm
Bob makes a quick life-saving decision that stabilizes a critical patient
Peter is called in from home and throws himself into surgical cases
Entire staff works through the night handling mass casualties

episodes.theme: the duality of medicine - calm and chaos

interactions.description: all storylines explore being tested under pressure - Carol's engagement doubts, Susan proving herself in crisis, Doug's professionalism despite heartbreak
the audience knows Doug still loves Carol while she's engaged to Tag, creating tension during their professional interactions
the mass casualty event serves as a crucible that tests all characters' medical abilities

events.event: Peter Benton publicizes organ availability before getting wife's consent
Wife discovers Benton's premature announcement and becomes furious
Wife refuses consent for organ harvest, eight recipients lose their chance
Six-year-old child revived after nearly drowning while ice fishing
Busload of choir kids treated for minor injuries after bus accident
Susan discovers Div Cvetic has completely disappeared
Carter gives Susan a piano-shaped music box as Christmas gift
Susan gently deflects Carter's romantic advance
Chloe's pregnancy becomes public knowledge
Doug crashes Carol's engagement party
Doug makes anguished declaration of love to Carol
Carol is shaken but does not reciprocate Doug's feelings
Tag confronts Doug and punches him in front of party guests
Doug wanders the streets alone on Christmas Eve

interactions.description: all storylines explore how professional focus and emotional walls prevent genuine human connection
Doug's declaration forces both his and Carol's storylines to intersect and change direction

events.event: Carter discovers a gunshot victim on the street while off-shift
Carter rushes into County General to alert Mark and the staff
Carol identifies the victim as a gang member dumped by rivals
Team rushes to victim's location and brings him back on stretcher
Mark teaches Carter how to intubate a patient in the field
Carter takes charge of intubating a helicopter trauma patient
Carter scrubs in for his first surgery with Benton's guidance
Susan confronts Kayson over Mr. Vennerbeck's case
Vennerbeck dies in surgery after returning with undiagnosed heart murmur
Kayson accuses Susan of withholding information and incomplete medical history
Kayson brings the matter before hospital administration
Chloe announces plans to move to Texas with boyfriend for the baby
Jackie insists Mae needs to be placed in a nursing home
Peter resists fiercely against nursing home placement

interactions.description: Carter, Susan, and Peter all face tests that could define their medical careers and personal lives
Carter's first surgery directly involves Benton as mentor, advancing both storylines

events.event: Carter is tasked with training new medical student Deb Chen
Deb Chen introduces herself to Carter and Benton, insisting on being called 'Deb'
Carter realizes Chen may be his rival for surgical sub-internship
Carol deals with emotionally challenging patient obsessed with colors
Mr. Desmond provokes ER staff as part of behavioral experiment
Boxer knocks out Mr. Desmond, ending his experiment
Kayson brings formal charges against Susan in Vennerbeck death
Morgenstern questions Susan's assertiveness, advises her to stand up for herself
Susan forced to prepare defense with limited institutional support
Peter and Walt search for missing Mae who wandered away
Benton realizes Mae cannot be left unsupervised but resists professional care
Carol announces wedding date to Tag
Carol pays more attention to Doug's behavior than wedding plans
Doug turns down woman at bar, staying faithful to Linda

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of responsibility - professional accountability, mentoring duties, family obligations, and commitment to relationships
the audience sees Carol's growing uncertainty about Tag while Doug commits to Linda, creating tension around their missed connection

events.event: Ms. Horne arrives with hematoma and bruises, claiming she fell off a ladder
Doug investigates further and questions the children about who hit their mother
Son David points finger at sister Mandy for hitting their mother
Mandy denies accusation but shows barely concealed rage
Susan appears before hospital review board to defend against Kayson's charges
Review board denies Kayson's reprimand against Susan
Kayson is brought to ER as patient after heart attack
Dr. Steinman wants angioplasty but frightened Kayson refuses
Susan threatens to bring Steinman before review board if he overrides patient wishes
Steinman backs down and grateful Kayson acknowledges Susan's assertiveness
Carter spends sleepless night preparing for presentation
Deb Chen delivers more polished presentation than Carter
Carter feels threatened by Chen's competence and questions if he belongs in surgery
Peter hires Jeanie Boulet as physical therapist to care for Mae
Peter's decision creates friction with sister Jackie who wasn't consulted
Doug treats homeless teenager with AIDS who has no resources
Woman who overdosed brought in with infant and suicide note
Carol and Haleh discuss what drives a mother to suicide

interactions.description: all storylines explore proving oneself worthy - Susan proving medical competence, Carter questioning his surgical belonging, Doug advocating for vulnerable patients
both storylines deal with protecting the vulnerable - Carol reflecting on maternal desperation, Doug investigating child abuse

events.event: Mark and Carter ride in ambulance to plane crash scene
Mark nonchalantly eats sandwich while Carter is jostled around
Mark and Carter intubate crash victim at scene
Morgenstern offers Mark attending physician position
Mark tells Jennifer about the job offer
Jennifer reacts with indignation, refuses to accept arrangement
Grace begs Mark to let her die rather than continue cancer treatment
Deb Chen delivers impressive presentation that wows Benton
Deb tells Carter she doesn't think he's cut out for surgery
Benton offends Haleh by trying to browbeat her into taking notes
Haleh retaliates by making Benton's day more difficult
Child with handgun enters ER looking to execute Yummy
Staff defuses gunman situation before anyone is harmed
Poisonous pet snake escapes in ER causing panic
New crash carts are stolen by cardiology department

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of medical careers - Mark's marriage suffers from his commitment, Carter questions if he belongs, Benton learns respect matters more than authority, staff proves competence under chaotic conditions
Mark mentors Carter in emergency medicine while his own personal life crumbles from the same dedication

events.event: Six-year-old Russian girl Tatiana is abandoned in the ER by her adoptive mother
Carol forms an immediate emotional bond with Tatiana, teaching Doug and Carter Russian phrases
Doug tells Carol the realistic outcome is antibiotics and foster care
Carol refuses to accept the realistic prognosis and spends extra time at Tatiana's bedside
Carol begins considering adoption despite her personal history complications
Mark and Susan treat a dog hit by Officer Grabarsky
The dog rescue becomes a bonding experience for the ER staff
Carter and Deb Chen treat three cheerleaders who ingested LSD-laced chocolates
Deb unknowingly eats contaminated candy and becomes impaired
Deb tells Carter she is 'very okay' while staring at a blank wall
Nurse Wendy fractures her hand treating seizure patient Michael
Impaired Deb wraps Wendy's hand in a comically oversized cast
Carol promises to fix Wendy's oversized cast
Married Dr. Kayson asks Susan to dinner romantically
Susan declines Kayson's advance, recognizing its inappropriateness

interactions.description: all storylines explore different aspects of caring - Carol's emotional investment in Tatiana, Susan's professional boundaries with Kayson, Carter learning to handle impaired colleagues
Doug and Carol's different approaches to Tatiana's case reveal their contrasting worldviews and relationship dynamic

events.event: Carter overhears Benton on phone about birthday party and assumes it's Benton's birthday
Carter tells Mark it's Benton's birthday, Mark mentions it's also Rachel's birthday
Carter organizes surprise celebration for Benton including belly dancers
Benton desperately tries to get someone to cover his shift for Mae's birthday
Dr. Angela Hicks berates Benton when he attempts to switch shifts
Benton misses his mother Mae's birthday party due to work obligations
Mark misses his daughter Rachel's birthday celebration
Doug learns little girl was injured by abusive father
Doug confronts and punches the abusive father Wilson in hospital lobby
Mark tells Doug that Wilson can't press charges due to child-abuse investigation
Morgenstern recommends Doug attend therapy for anger issues
Teenage girl Renee Franks brought in for drug overdose
Mr. Franks is dismissive when Mark discusses Renee's recurring problems
Renee wheeled back in after another overdose with shaken father finally grasping severity
Carol wants to adopt Tatiana but Tag is resistant to the idea
Carol visits group home and finds inadequate conditions for Tatiana
Jen accepts job in Milwaukee after Mark offered permanent attending position
Mark and Jen's career incompatibility fully exposed with neither willing to compromise

interactions.description: all storylines explore sacrifice of personal relationships for professional obligations
both Mark and Benton miss family birthday celebrations due to work demands
Carter's misunderstanding creates the situation that highlights both Mark's and Benton's family sacrifices

events.event: Peter trades two shifts to spend time with Mae and commits to a forty-eight-hour marathon shift
Dr. Hicks orders Peter to sleep but he refuses, pushing himself to dangerous exhaustion
Mae breaks her hip while Peter is at the hospital working his marathon shift
Peter learns Mae fell because Walt and Jackie were on vacation and no one was there to help
Carol learns her suicide attempt history makes her ineligible to adopt Tatiana
Devastated Carol goes to Doug in vulnerability, seeming to want to rekindle their connection
Doug refuses Carol and offers to drive her home to Tag instead
Doug notices Bonnie's star-shaped burn is inconsistent with mother's radiator story
Doug's investigation reveals Bonnie's mother deliberately burned her as punishment
Doug pursues the child abuse case aggressively
A man studying the ER offers Susan a job at a new hospital
Susan discovers the man is actually a psychiatric patient, not a hospital administrator
Jennifer tells Mark she wants a separation
Morgenstern informs Mark he's leaving for Harvard residency program

episodes.theme: the cost of ambition and isolation

interactions.description: all storylines explore how personal ambition and professional dedication isolate characters from meaningful connections
the audience sees Doug's growth and restraint while Carol remains unaware of his emotional development

patches.reason: Doug's love storyline covers both romantic development and child protection cases - these should be separate storylines

events.event: Jodi O'Brien arrives at ER with pregnancy symptoms, Mark diagnoses UTI and discharges her
Jodi collapses in parking lot and suffers seizure
Mark realizes his misdiagnosis - preeclampsia not UTI
With OB attending unavailable, Mark decides to deliver baby himself in ER
Jodi goes into active labor in ER after 45 minutes with no OB response
Baby suffers shoulder dystocia, gets stuck on Jodi's pubic bone
Mark manages to free infant and deliver living child
Jodi hemorrhages catastrophically during delivery
Despite frantic efforts, Jodi dies on the table
Dr. Coburn arrives too late and confronts Mark about his decisions
Sean O'Brien holds his newborn child, devastated by wife's death
Peter insists on scrubbing in for his mother Mae's hip surgery
Peter crosses professional line, unable to separate surgeon and son roles
Mark breaks down crying alone on subway ride home

episodes.theme: the weight of responsibility and the cost of caring

interactions.description: both Mark and Peter struggle with professional judgment compromised by personal stakes - Mark's career crisis mirrors his marriage crisis, Peter can't separate being a surgeon from being a son
episode shifts from standard multi-storyline format to single-patient focus as Jodi's case consumes Mark's attention

patches.reason: Susan storyline has no events in this episode despite being ranked B
Doug storyline has no events in this episode despite being ranked B
Carol storyline has no events in this episode despite being ranked B

events.event: Mark Greene is haunted by Jodi O'Brien's death and avoids seeing patients
Susan Lewis convinces Mark to take the night off and steps up to manage the ER
Susan treats a young man with hiccups, demonstrating diagnostic skill under pressure
Dr. Swift arrives unannounced as the new ER chief
Mark encounters Swift as a random cyclist at Doc Magoo's, not knowing he's the new boss
Swift runs an impromptu emergency drill at 2 AM, forcing Mark back to the hospital
Mark fails to impress Swift while Susan does well with the new chief
Mae Benton falls out of bed again after being released from restraints
Peter finally accepts Mae needs permanent nursing care
Peter asks Jeanie for help placing Mae in a facility
Carol learns Doug has a date with Diane Leeds and pretends not to be bothered
Carol remains unenthusiastic about planning her wedding to Tag
Carter and Deb Chen engage in increasingly absurd one-upmanship

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters confronting realities they've been avoiding - Mark's professional decline, Susan's readiness to lead, Peter's mother's needs, Carol's true feelings
the audience sees Mark's professional crisis while he remains unaware of how badly he's performing with Swift

patches.reason: Swift's arrival as new chief creates a new power dynamic storyline that doesn't fit existing character arcs

events.event: Mark leads a formal medical review of Jodi O'Brien's death from preeclampsia
Mark faces scrutiny from colleagues over his decisions during the delivery
Dr. Swift expresses concern about Mark's compromised judgment since the incident
Mark walks the board through his reasoning, exposing his misdiagnosis
Peter struggles to tell Mae she needs nursing home placement
Peter announces medical students' procedure books are due a week early
Deb and Carter race to log procedures before the deadline
Deb commits a nearly fatal error on a patient in her haste
Deb reconsiders whether competitive pressure is worth the risk
Deb leaves the program in the aftermath of her mistake
Chloe returns pregnant to live with Susan, abandoning Texas plans
Chloe's presence adds domestic chaos to Susan's professional life
Doug stumbles upon Jake at Diane's apartment
Jake reveals he already knows about Doug's overnight visits
Doug confronts what it means to be involved with a woman who has a child
Doug and Carter encounter a patient who refuses tuberculosis treatment

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of responsibility - professional accountability, family duty, personal obligation, parental commitment, and competitive ethics
Doug fears commitment while Carter witnesses the consequences of reckless ambition in Deb's departure

patches.reason: tuberculosis patient refusal raises public health vs autonomy questions that don't fit existing storylines

events.event: Peter approaches multiple surgeons about operating on a sixteen-year-old boy with a severe brain aneurysm
All surgeons refuse to take on the high-risk aneurysm case
Peter channels his frustration into advocacy for the patient as compensation for his inability to help his mother Mae
Peter and Jeanie go to dinner together, their relationship shifting from professional to personal
Susan tries to get Chloe to take her pregnancy seriously
Chloe ignores prenatal appointments and makes impulsive decisions
Susan becomes exhausted by constantly managing her sister while maintaining her career
Mark clashes with Dr. Swift over scheduling when he chooses family commitment over staying late
Swift questions whether Mark's personal problems are affecting his work
Carter makes a good impression on Swift, demonstrating initiative and competence
Doug turns down Linda Farrell's offer to celebrate together
Doug agrees to be assistant coach for Diane's son Jake's Little League team

episodes.theme: the helplessness of human planning against uncontrollable forces

interactions.description: all storylines explore how carefully laid plans collide with realities beyond control - Peter can't save the patient or his mother, Mark can't balance work and family, Susan can't control Chloe, Doug tries to break his pattern
Carter's success with Swift contrasts with Mark's deteriorating relationship with the new chief

events.event: Mark moves in with Jennifer in Milwaukee to save their marriage
Mark commutes to County General for his shifts from Milwaukee
Mark tries to rekindle romance by talking about happy memories
Jennifer remains resistant to Mark's romantic attempts
Doug coaches Jake's Little League team
Jake hits inside-the-park home run but misses first base
Doug lies to umpire about Jake touching first base
Carol and Doug treat Catholic high school basketball team with meningitis
Carol and Tag work on their wedding vows
Carol invites Diane Leeds to wedding knowing she'll bring Doug
Tag becomes upset about Doug's presence at the wedding
Malik finds magazine article about Carter's wealthy family

Carter decides to apply for both ER and surgical sub-internships
Benton's attraction to Jeanie Boulet continues to grow
Susan finds Cvetic's photo in cabbie's dating-service book
Chloe's irresponsibility continues to wear Susan down

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different forms of commitment and the sacrifices required to maintain them

events.event: Susan helps Chloe give birth to a baby girl
Cookie announces she's done helping and won't take in Chloe and the baby
Chloe shows no interest in caring for her newborn
Susan becomes suddenly responsible for the infant she didn't plan for

episodes.theme: the cost of responsibility and sacrifice

interactions.description: all storylines explore the weight of responsibility and the sacrifices demanded by duty, career, and family obligations
Susan is forced into motherhood while Carol prepares for marriage, both facing unwanted family responsibilities

events.event: Tag confronts Carol before the ceremony about her not loving him as much as he loves her
Carol admits Tag is right but insists she still wants to be with him
Tag calls off the wedding and becomes a runaway groom
Mark learns he is being sued for malpractice by Jodi O'Brien's family
Dr. Swift changes his mind and decides to support Mark as the new attending physician
Carter fills out a harsh evaluation of Peter Benton on his last day

Jake visits Doug at the hospital, reminding him of the family he lost

episodes.theme: the weight of choices and their consequences

interactions.description: all three storylines explore making difficult decisions that define one's future - Carol choosing between love and security, Carter choosing between honesty and career advancement, Susan accepting unwanted responsibility
Carol's wedding cancellation directly affects Doug's romantic storyline, as her choice frees her from Tag but doesn't resolve her feelings for Doug
the cancelled wedding reception becomes a symbolic gathering where all characters face their unresolved futures
